description: Upgrade packages/payments Razorpay and UPI services to use real razorpay npm SDK with fallback to in-memory simulation when no API keys configured
steps: Add razorpay npm package to packages/payments/package.json dependencies. Also add @types/crypto (or ensure crypto is available since Node 22 has it built-in - just need the HMAC). Check if razorpay package has types or needs @types/razorpay.

Rewrite packages/payments/src/services/razorpay-gateway.service.ts: Keep the same public API (RazorpayGateway class with createOrder, verifyPayment, createPayout, getPaymentStatus, getOrder, generateTestSignature). Add constructor config accepting key_id and key_secret from env (RAZORPAY_KEY_ID, RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET). When keys are present, use the razorpay SDK (new Razorpay({ key_id, key_secret })). For verifyPayment, implement real HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using Node crypto: hmac(orderId + '|' + paymentId, secret). When no keys configured, fall back to current in-memory simulation behavior. Export same schemas.

Rewrite packages/payments/src/services/upi-payment.service.ts: Keep the same public API (UPIPaymentService class with generatePaymentLink, verifyPayment, getPaymentStatus, expirePayment). Add constructor accepting Razorpay config (key_id, key_secret). When keys present, create orders via Razorpay with method='upi' and generate proper UPI deep links (upi://pay?pa=...). For verification, use Razorpay order status polling or signature verification. When no keys configured, fall back to current in-memory behavior.

Update existing tests in packages/payments/src/services/__tests__/razorpay-gateway.service.test.ts to cover both modes: Add tests for HMAC signature verification logic (can test the crypto function directly). Existing tests should continue to pass since they test in-memory mode (no env keys). Add a test that verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature computation is correct.

Add a test file packages/payments/src/services/__tests__/upi-payment.service.test.ts if one does not exist, covering: generatePaymentLink returns valid UPI deep link format, verifyPayment works in local mode, expirePayment works.
Run pnpm install to fetch the razorpay package.
Run pnpm typecheck to verify no TS errors.
Run pnpm test --filter=@quant/payments to verify payment tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: razorpay package is in packages/payments/package.json dependencies
RazorpayGateway uses real razorpay SDK when RAZORPAY_KEY_ID and RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET env vars are set
RazorpayGateway falls back to in-memory simulation when env vars are not set
verifyPayment uses real HMAC-SHA256(orderId|paymentId, key_secret) for signature verification in live mode
UPIPaymentService integrates with Razorpay UPI when credentials configured
UPIPaymentService falls back to in-memory when no credentials
All existing payment tests still pass
Same public API interface maintained (no breaking changes to exports)
verification: Run: pnpm install && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test --filter=@quant/payments
Verify: grep 'razorpay' packages/payments/package.json confirms dependency added
findings: razorpay npm package (v2.9.x) includes TypeScript types. The package uses CommonJS default export so import Razorpay from 'razorpay' works with esModuleInterop. Constructor now accepts optional config with DI client param for test mocking. Payouts remain simulated since they require RazorpayX separate API. All 389 existing tests pass unchanged. Prettier reformatted files during pre-commit hook.
